title: Empty collections should not be accessed or iterated
description: Empty collections should not be accessed or iteratedAnalyze your codeintentionality-logicalreliabilityBugsymbolic-executionWhy is this an issue?How can I fix it?When a collection is empty, iterating it has no effect. Doing so anyway is likely a bug; either population was accidentally omitted, or the iteration needs to be revised.Available In:Catch issues on the fly,in your IDEDetect issues in your GitHub, Azure DevOps Services, Bitbucket Cloud, GitLab repositoriesAnalyze code in youron-premise CI©2025SonarSource Sàrl. All rights reserved.Privacy Policy|Cookie Policy|Terms of Use
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

noncompliant:
var list = new List<int>();
var firstItem = list[0]; // Noncompliant - empty list access

logic.detection_conditions: Methods where empty collections are created and then accessed/iterated without checking if empty
logic.triggering_conditions: (creates empty collection via Enumerable.Empty, new List<T>(), or new T[0]) AND (accessed via [] or foreach) in same scope
logic.exclusion_conditions: Excludes collections initialized with items, collections from method calls, accesses guarded by count/any checks
logic.never_trigger_on: Collections from method calls, collections with initializers, accesses preceded by count/any checks
logic.reasoning: Accessing or iterating empty collections is pointless and likely indicates a bug. Detects simple patterns: var x = new List<T>(); x[0] or var x = Enumerable.Empty<T>(); foreach. This is a simplified check focusing on same-method obvious cases since full dataflow analysis requires semantic analysis. Targets MethodDeclaration nodes and looks for patterns where empty collection creation is followed by access.

should_trigger: var list = new List<int>(); var item = list[0];
var empty = Enumerable.Empty<string>(); foreach (var x in empty) { }
var arr = new string[0]; var item = arr[0];
should_not_trigger: var list = new List<int>(); if (list.Count > 0) { var item = list[0]; }
var collection = GetCollection(); foreach (var item in collection) { }
var list = new List<int> { 1, 2, 3 }; var item = list[0];

checks.message: Empty collection is accessed without checking if empty
Iterating known empty collection - remove foreach or populate collection
Iterating empty collection - check if collection has items first
Empty collection LINQ method will throw exception - check collection first
